column1: Fixed weekly activities declared at onboarding (day, type, intensity). The AI classifies free text into one commitment_type, the generator applies the numeric adjustments. volume_cut_sets applies across the muscles in volume_cut_muscles. adjacency_block_hours blocks Legs or Lower (or Pull, for climbing) that many hours either side. interval_slots_removed comes off the weekly conditioning budget. rir_penalty_next_day is added to rir_min on the following day. A commitment is training the app did not prescribe but the body still pays for.

column10: Never place a Legs or Lower day within adjacency_block_hours either side of the commitment.
Removes all standalone Interval work. The day after a match runs at rir_min plus rir_penalty_next_day.
Adjacency applies to hard or long runs only. Easy runs need no adjustment beyond the Interval slot removal.
No extra rotation core work in commitment weeks, the sport supplies it.
Low impact, low recovery cost. Only the Interval slot removal applies.
Cut Quads sets only when riding is hard or hilly, otherwise zero.
Never place a Pull day within adjacency_block_hours. Grip heavy exercises (deadlifts, carries, rows) move away from climbing days. Counts as half an Interval session, round down.
Removes all standalone Interval work. Volume cut applies to whichever muscle the user reports as most worked.
Adjacency applies before performances and hard rehearsals.
Start weekly volume at the bottom of the goal's range. Apply F03 after long shifts the user flags.

column2: Casual grade, under 1 hour, low intensity
column10: Counts toward general activity only. No adjustment.
Default: treat as full body conditioning, ask the user which muscles it works, then reclassify into a row above.
